feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Datasphere
feed.subtitle: SAP Datasphere Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How to transport HANA DB tables and procedures from an Open SQL schema of SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi,

We have Datasphere on the Business Data Cloud, and we are using JDBC to bring data into Datasphere.

In a SPACE we have created Tables using the HANA Database Explorer and we will be creating Stored Procedures as well and more in the HANA Database Explorer of the HANA DB Service linked to our SPACE.

Question:

When we have a SPACE and we are using the HANA Database behind the SPACE, 

how do we "Transport" artefacts that we create in the HANA Database which is linked to our SPACE ?

We cannot find any documentation explaining how to Transport artefacts created in the HANA Database Explorer of our SPACE, and hence please point us to the SAP documentation on this.

Thank you and best regards.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Datasphere Replication
entry.content:
Hi, 

Can we use below CDS Views for Datasphere replication, although it meets the required annotations for replication, I saw a comment in CDS View saying 
Unfortunately, only views with a prefix 'I_' and 'C_' are allowed to be released for C1 contract delta.changeDataCapture.automatic: true --- Could you please clarify

@analytics.dataExtraction: { enabled: true, // Unfortunately only views with a prefix 'I_' and 'C_' are allowed to be released for C1 contract delta.changeDataCapture.automatic: true}

 

VC_INTEGRATION_DD03L           Extraction for DD03L
VC_INTEGRATION_DD04T           Extraction for DD04T
VC_INTEGRATION_DD07T           Extraction view for DD07T
VC_INTEGRATION_DD07T_CPS       Extraction view for DD07T
VC_INTEGRATION_EWUKEYS         Extraction for EWUKEYS
VC_INTEGRATION_IBIN            Extraction for IBIN
VC_INTEGRATION_IBINVALUES      Extraction for IBINVALUES
VC_INTEGRATION_IBSYMBOL        Extraction for IBSYMBOL
VC_INTEGRATION_INOB            Extraction for INOB
VC_INTEGRATION_KLAH            Extraction for KLAH
VC_INTEGRATION_KNA1            Extraction for KNA1
VC_INTEGRATION_KNVV            Extraction for KNVV
VC_INTEGRATION_KSML            Extraction for KSML
VC_INTEGRATION_KSSK

entry.title: Read all roles from Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello, Is there API to read all roles from Datasphere?

or alternate way to read all roles from Datasphere

entry.title: Datasphere: Replication Flow on Space-Type HANA Data Lake Files
entry.content:
Hello SAP-Community,

we have the following issue:

On space Type SAP HANA Data Lake Files, an attempt is being made to create a Replication Flow for BW4/HANA source object ADSO with primary key. During deployment, the following error message is displayed:

"Load type not supportedSource object: ZRCCONTID$FThe Delta Only and Initial and Delta load types are not supported for SAP ECC or SAP BW source objects. "

Do any settings need to be adjusted in the space configuration? Has anyone else experienced this issue before?
 
The replication Flow - Load-Type is: Initial Only
 
Our system is also based on SAP BW/4HANA

Thanks for the support

Best regards, 

David

entry.title: Guidence on Standard CDS View Equivalents for SAP HCM BW Extractors
entry.content:
We are currently migrating SAP BW extractors to SAP S/4HANA CDS Views for SAP Datasphere integration.

During the assessment, we observed that several standard SAP HCM BW extractors are difficult to replace with standard CDS Views because these extractors contain extensive business logic implemented through multiple standard SAP function modules, includes, and ABAP routines.

The extractors derive and calculate numerous fields at runtime. These derived and calculated columns are not directly available in the underlying database tables, and the corresponding business logic is not exposed through standard CDS Views.

As a result, recreating the same functionality in CDS Views would require significant custom development and may not produce results identical to the standard BW extractor.

The below extractors :

0HR_PA_1 0HR_PA_00HR_PA_OS_10HR_PY_10HR_PA_BN_1

Request: Please clarify the following:

  1. Does SAP provide standard CDS View equivalents for the affected HCM BW extracto

entry.title: is a direct JDBC connection from MS Fabric to SAP Datasphere compliant?
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I found this video that claim that a JDBC connection from Datasphere to MS Fabric is compliant to extract the data.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VLYk8FW15ng

From what I understand the JDBC / ODBC connection to extract data to a third party data platform is out of SUR of Datasphere.

What is the truth? am I missing something?

Best Regards,

Alberto

entry.title: connection between SAP Datasphere and Microsoft Fabric
entry.content:
Hello SAP team,

I am SAP Datapshere admin and would like to configure connection between SAP Datasphere and Microsoft Fabric. Can I ask you if this is supported by Datasphere ? I can't find any information about this connection type. If it's supported then what connection type could be used for that ? Customer requested connection to Azure SQL DB mirrored to MS Fabric.

 

Thanks.

 

Miroslav Conka

SAP Datasphere admin

entry.title: Alternative to ChangeDocumentCollection for Service Request checklist change history in SSC
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

we need to extract checklist change history for Service Requests from SAP Service Cloud into SAP Datasphere.
Currently we use: /sap/c4c/odata/v1/changedoclist/ChangeDocumentCollection

with the mandatory filter: BusinessObject eq 'ServiceRequest'
and a date filter such as: ChangeDateTime ge datetime'2025-11-01T00:00:00'

The service provides the information we need, especially old/new values and change metadata:
ChangeDateTime
ObjectNodeElementName
ObjectNodeElementOldContent
ObjectNodeElementNewContent

However, the extraction is very slow and runs into timeouts in SAP Datasphere. 

Extracting the data with date filter such as: ChangeDateTime ge datetime'2026-06-01T00:00:00' is running successful, but we need the data starting from 2025-11-01T00:00:00'.

Is there a better OData service/entity for Service Request checklist change history?
Or is ChangeDocumentCollection the only standard source for old/new value changes?

Is there a supported way to apply a monthly date range filter on ChangeDateTime in the ChangeDocumentCollection?

Thanks!

Best regards
Julian

SAP Datasphere OData SAP Service Cloud

entry.title: How to improve performance for federated data in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello DSP Experts,

I am facing a challenge in DSP as the data is not rendering in SAC story while I am using the live data from the source but it's working fine with persistence data. In the model we are supposed to have atleast 13 months of records as per the requirement so this way the volume of data is heavy. All possible solutions have been implemented like filters , joining conditions to reduce the data volume. So is there any other way which we can implement from a different perspectives to optimise the performance in Datasphere. And as per the policy we are not supposed to use the persisted data.

entry.title: How to optimise query performance in sap datasphere if my source is service now?
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

Could you share any recommendations or best practices for improving performance in SAP Datasphere without persisting the data? We are looking for alternative approaches that can help optimize query performance while keeping the data virtualized.

entry.title: Data Sphere Package assignment issue for the objects
entry.content:
hi Team,

In Data sphere development, though the objects are under package, when we open that particular object package assignment showing as "None". For example, data sphere view Y2_VG_DI_CROS_PROFCNTR_001 is available under package PROFITCENTER_MD. But when we open the object package showing as "None". Because of this issue, when the object is already moved to production, if there are any changes to the objects in DEV it is difficult to find the actual package where the object is already available.

Please check the issue.

 

Thanks

Ramya

entry.title: SAC date variable input as MM/DD/YYYY?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Business users want to enter values for a date variable in MM/DD/YYYY format in SAC story (built on Datasphere models) and I’m looking for guidance regarding date-variable input formatting.

Context:

  • Data is loaded from S/4HANA into the Datasphere Raw layer. Date fields initially arrive as strings in YYYYMMDD format (e.g., 20251231). We manually change the data type of date fields to the "Date".
  • In the Harmonization layer, we consume the Raw layer table without transformations.
  • In the Business layer, we apply cleansing, business logic, master data alignment, and assign semantics to date fields as Calendar Date.
  • In the Consumption layer, we build an Analytic Model and create variables on these date fields.
  • The SAC story is based on this Analytical Model, and the date variables appear as designed
  • The SAC user profile is configured to display dates in MM/DD/YYYY format.

Issue:

When running the story:
  • The variable's value help (possible values) shows the description in MM/DD/YYYY format and the ID displays in YYYY-MM-DD format. User can search in MM/DD/YYYY then select it and the variable show MM/DD/YYYY format (in form of description but accepts the entry as YYYY-MM-DD)
  • If the user manually enters a date as MM/DD/YYYY (without selecting from the value help), SAC throws an error: “Member ID does not exist”.
 

I understand that variables accept the key value rather than the description, but I want to know:

 

Is there any standard SAP-supported way for SAC variables to accept date input in MM/DD/YYYY format, without creating custom fields, casts, or transformations? Since we have many date fields, implementing custom logic repeatedly would be costly, so we’re hoping for a standard approach if one exists.

Thanks in advance for your insights!

Blogs referred:
Assistance Required – Date Format Handling in Data... - SAP Community

Solved: Date shown wrong in SAP Analytics Cloud based on S... - SAP Community

Regards,

Preet

entry.title: Power BI Refresh Failure When S/4HANA Is Unavailable Despite Persisted Data in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
We have the following data flow:

SAP S/4HANA (On-Premise) → SAP Datasphere (CDS Remote Table) → Persisted Data → Power BI

The data from the CDS Remote Table has been successfully persisted in SAP Datasphere. However, when the SAP S/4HANA source system is unavailable, the Power BI dataset refresh fails.

Expected Behavior:
Since the data is already persisted in SAP Datasphere, Power BI should be able to refresh successfully by reading the persisted data without requiring connectivity to the SAP S/4HANA source system.

Actual Behavior:
When SAP S/4HANA is unavailable, the Power BI refresh fails, indicating that there is still a dependency on the source system even though the data has been persisted.

Request:
Please help us understand:

* Why does the Power BI refresh still depend on SAP S/4HANA after the data has been persisted in SAP Datasphere?
* Is this expected behavior for persisted data based on CDS Remote Tables?
* Are there any additional configurations or best practices required to ensure that Power BI reads only from the persisted data without requiring connectivity to the source system?

Please advise on the root cause and the

entry.title: MRPELEMENT_OPEN_QUANTITY mismatch between MD04 and YY1_SUPDEMANDITEMS for same material and plant
entry.content:
Hello Team, we observed a discrepancy between the quantity displayed in Transaction MD04 and the value returned by the custom CDS View YY1_SUPDEMANDITEMS for the field MRPELEMENT_OPEN_QUANTITY.
System Details:
S/4HANA
Material: BSA1007208401
Plant: 4060
Period: July 2026

Observed Values:
MD04 Requirement Quantity: 1281
YY1_SUPDEMANDITEMS.MRPELEMENT_OPEN_QUANTITY: 1460
We expected both values to be the same, but they differ by 179.

Could anyone help us understand:

1. How is MRPELEMENT_OPEN_QUANTITY calculated in YY1_SUPDEMANDITEMS..?
2. Which standard CDS views or database tables are used to derive this value?
3. Does this field include additional MRP elements (planned orders, purchase requisitions, purchase orders, reservations, etc.) that are not reflected in the MD04 Requirement quantity?
4. Is this difference expected as part of standard SAP functionality?

Any clarification on the standard calculation logic would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Is there an option to use the cdsview I_ACTUALPLANJOURNALENTRYITEM, but with Delta extraction ?
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

I need a CDSView to use in DataSphere, I will replace the BW 0CO_OM_CCA_9(COVP, COSP Tables) and 0CO_OM_OPA_6(COEP Table).

I thought about using I_ACTUALPLANJOURNALENTRYITEM(ACDOCA), but there's no delta capture in that CDSView.

Thanks.

Regards.

entry.title: Datasphere to SF ECP systems connection
entry.content:
Hi,

Can you please help us to create connection between Datasphere and SF ECP systems. 

Thanks.

entry.title: 2LIS_03_BX and 2LIS_03_BF equivalent CDS views in S/4
entry.content: We were using 2LIS_03_BX and 2LIS_03_BF ODP extractor to calculate the Inventory at all the attributes level to create a analytics product in our BW analytics platform. when come to S/4 system, covering all the important fields what the equivalent CDS views?. As per SAP recommendation we need to use the CDS views for data extraction. please provide your best recommendation.

entry.title: Silent Failure in SAP Datasphere Replication Flows - A Real Production Gap Nobody Talks About
entry.content:
While working on a live SAP BDC project, I noticed a critical monitoring gap in Replication Flows that I haven't seen documented anywhere clearly.

The Problem

When a table inside a Replication Flow gets stuck in "Retrying" state during Initial and Delta load, the Replication Flow itself shows no error, no warning, no alert. It simply shows retry count — sometimes 2000+ retries — and continues silently. 

From the outside, the flow looks completely healthy. This is not specific to one source — whether you use S/4HANA, SLT, SuccessFactors or any other source, the same gap exists. You only discover the issue when you: Manually open each Replication Flow and drill into each individual table inside it and check the latest loaded timestamp manually in Data Integration Monitor.

The Scale Problem

In a real enterprise BDC project, you can easily have 300+ Replication Flows, each containing multiple tables. Nobody can realistically open every flow and check every table daily in Data Integration Monitor.

The result? I only found out a table had been silently retrying for hours when either manually checking each flow daily or the reporting/KPI team flagged that their data wasn't populating. The problem had already reached downstream before anyone on the data team knew.

My Suggestion to SAP Team

A configurable smart threshold option per Replication Flow — with two options:

Time based — if no successful load has happened in X hours since the last successful load, mark as Failed and trigger alert

Count based — if consecutive retry count since last successful load exceeds X times, mark as Failed and trigger alert

The key word here is since last successful load. In Initial and Delta load, retrying between successful loads is normal expected behavior. So, the threshold should not count total retries ever — it should reset the counter every time a table loads successfully and only trigger an alert when retries keep happening with no successful load in between.

This is a small but critical distinction that makes the feature actually usable for delta replication scenarios.

Almost every enterprise ETL tool has this capability. For a platform like BDC targeting large enterprise deployments, this monitoring gap needs to be addressed.

Has anyone else faced this? Would love to know if you found a workaround?

@SAP SAP Datasphere SAP Business Data Cloud

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA PCE Migration: Datasphere vs. BODS with Azure – Architecture Recommendations
entry.content:
Hi

My organization has begun its SAP S/4HANA PCE migration. As part of our Phase 2 roadmap, we are evaluating between SAP Datasphere and SAP BusinessObjects Data Services (BODS) with Microsoft Azure serving as the data storage layer.

SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) will be our enterprise reporting and analytics solution.

FYI, we do not currently have an enterprise data warehouse.

Given this landscape, I would appreciate the community's insights and recommendations on the following topics:

  • Does BODS support extraction of SAP S/4HANA CDS views? If so, are there any limitations or best practices?
  • How well does BODS integrate with Azure-based data storage for SAP data?
  • How do the licensing costs compare between SAP Datasphere (including storage) and a BODS + Azure storage architecture?
  • What capabilities or features would we be giving up by choosing BODS with Azure storage instead of SAP Datasphere?
  • Are there any other architectural, operational, or long-term considerations that we should take into account before making this decision?

Any experiences, recommendations, or lessons learned from similar implementations would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Clarification on Using BW DataSources in Datasphere Migration
entry.content:
Hello SAP Team,

We are currently migrating our BW data flows to SAP Datasphere.

During this migration, we have encountered a challenge in identifying appropriate CDS views for certain objects. Some of the required fields are custom fields that were previously used in BW DSOs, and for these cases we are currently relying on BW DataSources (For Example: 0HR_PA_0, 0HR_PA_1, 0HRPOSITION_ATTR and 0EC_PCA_1). 

Can we use these Datasources or do we need to create CDS views ? Which one is preferable ?

However, we are unable to identify equivalent CDS views or the corresponding underlying tables for some of these fields.

Could you please confirm whether using BW DataSources in such scenarios is considered a recommended or acceptable best practice when equivalent CDS views are not available?

Additionally, if it is acceptable to use BW DataSources in these cases, could you also confirm whether the same approach can be applied consistently across other functional modules where similar limitations exist?

We would appreciate your guidance on this matter.

Thank you in advance for your support.

 

Regards,
Radhakrushna
